proven.claim: PROVEN: the console.log is only reachable when vacuousCount > 0
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 126
; PROVEN: the console.log is only reachable when vacuousCount > 0
(declare-const vacuous_count Int)
(declare-const log_executed Int)      ; 1 = log line reached
; Transition: if (vacuousCount > 0) { console.log(...) }
; log_executed = 1 implies the guard was true
(assert (=> (= log_executed 1) (> vacuous_count 0)))
; Precondition: log is executing (we're at line 126)
(assert (= log_executed 1))
; Negated property: log executes but vacuousCount <= 0
(assert (not (> vacuous_count 0)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: REACHABLE: log claim "N vacuous blocks filtered" can undercount genuinely vacuous blocks
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 126
; REACHABLE: log claim "N vacuous blocks filtered" can undercount genuinely vacuous blocks
(declare-const truly_vacuous Int)
(declare-const is_vacuous_detected Int)
(declare-const vacuous_count Int)
; Precondition: at least one block is genuinely vacuous (no multi-variable transitions)
(assert (>= truly_vacuous 1))
; Transition: vacuous_count = blocks.filter(isVacuous).length
; From existing violations: isVacuous returns false when declared-var name is an SMT-LIB
; keyword, when a variable reference appears only in an inline comment, or when
; assertions span multiple lines (only the opening line is scanned)
(assert (>= is_vacuous_detected 0))
(assert (< is_vacuous_detected truly_vacuous))
; Transition: vacuous_count is exactly what isVacuous detected, not ground truth
(assert (= vacuous_count is_vacuous_detected))
; Violation: log reports fewer filtered blocks than actually vacuous;
; misclassified vacuous blocks flow into verifyBlock unchallenged
(assert (< vacuous_count truly_vacuous))
(check-sat)

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 126
; PROVEN: the console.log is only reachable when vacuousCount > 0
(declare-const vacuous_count Int)
(declare-const log_executed Int)      ; 1 = log line reached
; Transition: if (vacuousCount > 0) { console.log(...) }
; log_executed = 1 implies the guard was true
(assert (=> (= log_executed 1) (> vacuous_count 0)))
; Precondition: log is executing (we're at line 126)
(assert (= log_executed 1))
; Negated property: log executes but vacuousCount <= 0
(assert (not (> vacuous_count 0)))
(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 126
; REACHABLE: log claim "N vacuous blocks filtered" can undercount genuinely vacuous blocks
(declare-const truly_vacuous Int)
(declare-const is_vacuous_detected Int)
(declare-const vacuous_count Int)
; Precondition: at least one block is genuinely vacuous (no multi-variable transitions)
(assert (>= truly_vacuous 1))
; Transition: vacuous_count = blocks.filter(isVacuous).length
; From existing violations: isVacuous returns false when declared-var name is an SMT-LIB
; keyword, when a variable reference appears only in an inline comment, or when
; assertions span multiple lines (only the opening line is scanned)
(assert (>= is_vacuous_detected 0))
(assert (< is_vacuous_detected truly_vacuous))
; Transition: vacuous_count is exactly what isVacuous detected, not ground truth
(assert (= vacuous_count is_vacuous_detected))
; Violation: log reports fewer filtered blocks than actually vacuous;
; misclassified vacuous blocks flow into verifyBlock unchallenged
(assert (< vacuous_count truly_vacuous))
(check-sat)
